title: Request & Response Pattern — NXGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: Contract types define the API surface. DTOs (mutable, `{ get; set; }`) are the response shape for GET endpoints and the base class for SaveRequests. SaveRequests inherit from the entity DTO (`SaveXyzRequest : XyzDTO`) and add only write-specific fields — most are empty. DTOs inherit from `KeyedDTO` which provides the `Guid Id` property (`Guid.Empty` = create). All contract types live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`. See [ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)...

Does the entity have Id (Guid), Name (string), Description (string?) as direct properties?
|-- YES -> inherit NamedDTO  (e.g., LanguageDTO : NamedDTO)
+-- NO  -> inherit KeyedDTO
         +-- Name field exists but called something else? -> KeyedDTO
         +-- Has extra base fields beyond Name/Description? -> KeyedDTO

rules.rule_text: **SaveRequest inherits from entity DTO** — `SaveXyzRequest : XyzDTO`
**`Guid Id = Guid.Empty`** via `KeyedDTO` — `Guid.Empty` means create, non-empty means update
**DTOs use `{ get; set; }`** — mutable for the roundtrip pattern
**All entity DTOs inherit `KeyedDTO` or `NamedDTO`** — never define `Guid Id` directly
**Delete requests use `Guid[] EntityKeys`** for batch support
**All DTOs, Requests, Responses live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`** — use namespace `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts.{Feature}` (e.g., `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts.Actions`)
**DTO suffix is ALL CAPS** — `ActionDTO`, not `ActionDto`
**All DTOs are classes** (not sealed records)
**Validate all inputs** — FluentValidation for every mutating endpoint
**Grid item DTOs are flat** — no nested objects, no child collections
**Use EnumDTO** for all enum/status/kind/mode fields on DTOs ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
**Use NamedDTO** for all FK references the UI displays by name ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
**Use C# enums** for all status/kind/mode fields — never `const string` ([ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md))
**Separate child request types** — `Save{Child}Request : {Child}DTO`
**EnumDTO roundtrip** — SaveRequest inherits EnumDTO fields from DTO; EntityMapper reads `.Id` and casts to C# enum
**NamedDTO roundtrip** — SaveRequest inherits NamedDTO fields from DTO; EntityMapper reads `.Id`
**Inherit NamedDTO** when the entity DTO has `{Id, Name, Description?}` as base fields
**Use `string?` for optional** and `string = string.Empty` for required
**Initialize collections** — `List<T> Prop { get; set; } = []`
**Initialize NamedDTO/EnumDTO to `null!`** — not `new()` (avoid empty default objects in serialization)
**Return `SaveResponse(Guid Id)`** from save endpoints
**Use `sealed record`** for save responses, delete requests, and lookup DTOs
**Return `NamedDTO` directly** from lookup endpoints when `{Id, Name, Description?}` suffices
**Empty SaveRequest** — most SaveRequests are empty classes; only add write-specific fields not on the DTO
**Create duplicate type definitions across projects** — each type has exactly one definition
**Define SaveRequest as independent type** — always inherit from entity DTO
**Use `Guid? Id`** for create detection — use `Guid Id = Guid.Empty` from `KeyedDTO`
**Use `{ get; init; }`** on DTOs — DTOs are mutable (`{ get; set; }`)
**Use `[JsonIgnore] public new`** to hide fields — the EntityMapper ignores server-computed fields
**Use C# enum directly in SaveRequests** — inherit `EnumDTO` from DTO, mapper casts `.Id`
**Use raw `Guid` for FK references in SaveRequests** — inherit `NamedDTO` from DTO, mapper reads `.Id`
**Expose navigation properties** in DTOs — map to NamedDTO or EnumDTO
**Use EF entities** as request/response types
**Define contract types in `Nxgn.Actions.Api`** — they belong in Contracts
**Use nested objects in grid item DTOs** — flatten to scalar fields
**Use lowercase `Dto` suffix** — always `DTO`
**Use `const string` classes** for status/kind fields — use C# enums ([ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md))
**Define `Guid Id` directly on DTOs** — inherit from `KeyedDTO` or `NamedDTO`

sections.title: Base Types
content: Base class for all entity DTOs. Provides the `Guid Id` property used for create/update detection.
public Guid Id { get; set; } = Guid.Empty;  // Guid.Empty = create
Three usages: FK reference field, base class for entity DTOs, and direct lookup return value. Inherits from `KeyedDTO`.
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;

**When to inherit NamedDTO**: When the entity DTO has `Id`, `Name`, and optionally `Description` as its base fields. If the entity uses different field names (e.g., `ReferenceNumber`, `Code`) or lacks `Name`, inherit `KeyedDTO` directly.
| **FK reference** | `public NamedDTO WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;` on ActionDTO |
| **Lookup return** | Return `List<NamedDTO>` from a lookup endpoint |
For all enum/classification fields — enables direct dropdown binding with zero frontend transformation ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)).
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public EnumDTO(int id, string name) { Id = id; Name = name; }
Usage: `public EnumDTO Status { get; set; } = null!;`

**Roundtrip pattern**: GET returns `EnumDTO { Id = 1, Name = "InProgress" }` -> frontend binds directly to dropdown -> Save sends the same `EnumDTO` back -> EntityMapper reads `.Id` and casts to C# enum.

sections.title: Entity DTOs
content: Full shape for GET responses. DTOs use `{ get; set; }` — they are mutable for the roundtrip pattern (GET -> mutate -> POST as SaveRequest). ALL DTOs are classes (not sealed records). ALL entity DTOs inherit from `KeyedDTO` or `NamedDTO`.
// Inherits NamedDTO — Name/Description come from translations (resolved by DTOMapper via TranslationService)
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public EnumDTO Status { get; set; } = null!;
public EnumDTO Priority { get; set; } = null!;
public NamedDTO WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
// Inherits NamedDTO — has Id, Name, Description (from translations)
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
// Inherits NamedDTO — has Id, Name, Description (from translations)
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
Does the entity have translated Name/Description (via ITranslatedEntity<T>)?
|-- YES -> inherit NamedDTO  (e.g., ActionDTO : NamedDTO, ComplianceProfileDTO : NamedDTO)
|          Name/Description filled by DTOMapper via TranslationService
Does the entity have Id (Guid), Name (string), Description (string?) as direct properties?
+-- Name field exists but called something else? -> KeyedDTO
+-- Has extra base fields beyond Name/Description? -> KeyedDTO

All status, kind, and mode fields use C# enums on the entity and `EnumDTO` on the DTO. Since SaveRequest inherits from the DTO, it also carries `EnumDTO` fields. The mapper reads `.Id` and casts to the C# enum.
// Entity (C# enum with HasConversion<string>() in EF)
public ActionStatus Status { get; set; } = ActionStatus.Open;
public EnumDTO Status { get; set; } = null!;
// SaveRequest inherits DTO — same EnumDTO field comes through
All FK references the UI displays by name use `NamedDTO` on the DTO. Since SaveRequest inherits from the DTO, it also carries `NamedDTO` fields. The mapper reads `.Id` from the NamedDTO.
public WorkflowDefinition WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
public NamedDTO WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
// SaveRequest inherits DTO — same NamedDTO field comes through
WorkflowDefinition = new NamedDTO { Id = entity.WorkflowDefinition.Id, Name = translatedName }

sections.title: Save Requests
content: SaveRequest **inherits from the entity DTO** — it is the same shape with optional write-specific fields added. The roundtrip is: GET returns DTO -> client mutates fields -> POST as SaveRequest (which IS-A DTO). The `Guid Id` from `KeyedDTO` distinguishes create (`Guid.Empty`) from update (non-empty). Most SaveRequests are empty classes.
// Empty — all writable fields come from ActionDTO
// Adds write-specific fields not on the DTO
public List<SaveWorkflowStateRequest> SaveStates { get; set; } = [];  // Child saves for diffing
1. **GET endpoint** returns the entity DTO (e.g., `ActionDTO` with `Id`, `ReferenceNumber`, `Status`, `Evidence`, etc.)
2. **Frontend** receives the DTO, user edits fields
4. **EntityMapper** reads only the writable fields, ignores server-computed fields (`CreatedDate`, counts, etc.)
5. **Server-computed fields** (`CreatedDate`, counts, `Version`) are present on the request body but **ignored by the EntityMapper** — the mapper simply does not read them
// KeyedDTO provides: public Guid Id { get; set; } = Guid.Empty;
When a parent has child collections, define a separate `Save{Child}Request` that inherits from the child DTO:
| Fields the caller writes directly | Server-computed (`CreatedDate`, `UpdatedDate`, counts) |
| FK references via `.Id` from NamedDTO | Read-only aggregations (`ActionCount`) |
| Child collections as `Save{Child}Request` | Fields derived from route/context |
| Enum fields via `.Id` from EnumDTO, cast to C# enum | Status (if managed by lifecycle endpoints) |
// CORRECT — inherits DTO, Guid.Empty for create
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;
// Duplicates DTO fields, drifts out of sync
// CORRECT — SaveRequest inherits EnumDTO from DTO, mapper casts .Id
// WRONG — separate enum field on request
public ActionStatus Status { get; set; }  // Don't do this
// CORRECT — SaveRequest inherits NamedDTO from DTO, mapper reads .Id
public Guid WorkflowDefinitionId { get; set; }  // Don't do this

sections.title: Save Responses
content: Return the created/updated entity ID plus any one-time side-effect data. Save responses are `sealed record` types.

sections.title: Delete Requests
content: Always use `EntityKeys[]` to support batch deletion. Delete requests are `sealed record` types.

sections.title: Grid Item DTOs
content: Flattened DTOs for list/grid endpoints. FK references are flattened to scalar fields (e.g., `WorkflowName` not `WorkflowDefinition.Name`). Enum references are flattened to `{Field}Name` string. Grid rows never contain nested objects or child collections. Grid item DTOs use `{ get; set; }` and inherit from `KeyedDTO`.
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string StatusName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string PriorityName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string WorkflowName { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
| DTO field type | Grid item flattening |

sections.title: Lookup DTOs
content: Minimal DTOs for dropdown/autocomplete endpoints. When the lookup shape is exactly `{ Id, Name, Description? }`, return `NamedDTO` directly. When extra fields are needed, define a `{Entity}LookupItemDTO`:
// Lookup returns NamedDTO directly — no custom type needed
public sealed record WorkflowLookupItemDTO(Guid Id, string Name, string Code, bool IsActive);

sections.title: Enum Lookup Endpoints
content: Expose lookup endpoints for each enum type returning `List<EnumDTO>` ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md)):

sections.title: GridListResponse
content: Standard wrapper for all grid/list endpoints. Uses `sealed record` with `IReadOnlyList<T>`.

sections.title: PagedRequest
content: Standard request type for all grid/list endpoints.

sections.title: Validators
content: Every mutating endpoint gets a FastEndpoints validator. Validators live in the API project (not Contracts) and reference Contracts types.

sections.title: JSON Conventions
content: | **Enums** | String values in JSON (`"Active"`, not `0`) via `JsonStringEnumConverter` |

sections.title: Frontend Model
content: Frontend models match the backend DTO shape. Since SaveRequest inherits DTO, the frontend sends the same shape for both read and write:
// DTO — used for both read and write
status: { id: number; name: string };                // EnumDTO
priority: { id: number; name: string };              // EnumDTO
workflowDefinition: { id: string; name: string };    // NamedDTO
currentState: { id: string; name: string } | null;   // NamedDTO
// Frontend sends: POST /api/v1/actions with the full DTO shape
// Backend EntityMapper reads only the writable fields, ignores the rest

sections.title: Rules
content: 2. **`Guid Id = Guid.Empty`** via `KeyedDTO` — `Guid.Empty` means create, non-empty means update
3. **DTOs use `{ get; set; }`** — mutable for the roundtrip pattern
4. **All entity DTOs inherit `KeyedDTO` or `NamedDTO`** — never define `Guid Id` directly
5. **Delete requests use `Guid[] EntityKeys`** for batch support
6. **All DTOs, Requests, Responses live in `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts`** — use namespace `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts.{Feature}` (e.g., `Nxgn.Actions.Contracts.Actions`)
9. **Validate all inputs** — FluentValidation for every mutating endpoint
10. **Grid item DTOs are flat** — no nested objects, no child collections
11. **Use EnumDTO** for all enum/status/kind/mode fields on DTOs ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
12. **Use NamedDTO** for all FK references the UI displays by name ([ADR-0005](../../adr/0005-enumdto-nameddto-for-ui-binding.md))
13. **Use C# enums** for all status/kind/mode fields — never `const string` ([ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md))
15. **EnumDTO roundtrip** — SaveRequest inherits EnumDTO fields from DTO; EntityMapper reads `.Id` and casts to C# enum
16. **NamedDTO roundtrip** — SaveRequest inherits NamedDTO fields from DTO; EntityMapper reads `.Id`
1. **Inherit NamedDTO** when the entity DTO has `{Id, Name, Description?}` as base fields
2. **Use `string?` for optional** and `string = string.Empty` for required
4. **Initialize NamedDTO/EnumDTO to `null!`** — not `new()` (avoid empty default objects in serialization)
6. **Use `sealed record`** for save responses, delete requests, and lookup DTOs
7. **Return `NamedDTO` directly** from lookup endpoints when `{Id, Name, Description?}` suffices
8. **Empty SaveRequest** — most SaveRequests are empty classes; only add write-specific fields not on the DTO
1. **Create duplicate type definitions across projects** — each type has exactly one definition
2. **Define SaveRequest as independent type** — always inherit from entity DTO
3. **Use `Guid? Id`** for create detection — use `Guid Id = Guid.Empty` from `KeyedDTO`
4. **Use `{ get; init; }`** on DTOs — DTOs are mutable (`{ get; set; }`)
5. **Use `[JsonIgnore] public new`** to hide fields — the EntityMapper ignores server-computed fields
6. **Use C# enum directly in SaveRequests** — inherit `EnumDTO` from DTO, mapper casts `.Id`
7. **Use raw `Guid` for FK references in SaveRequests** — inherit `NamedDTO` from DTO, mapper reads `.Id`
8. **Expose navigation properties** in DTOs — map to NamedDTO or EnumDTO
10. **Define contract types in `Nxgn.Actions.Api`** — they belong in Contracts
11. **Use nested objects in grid item DTOs** — flatten to scalar fields
13. **Use `const string` classes** for status/kind fields — use C# enums ([ADR-0004](../../adr/0004-enum-over-string-constants-for-status-fields.md))
14. **Define `Guid Id` directly on DTOs** — inherit from `KeyedDTO` or `NamedDTO`

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | GridListResponse | Per-entity response class with `GridRows` + `TotalCount` | Generic `GridListResponse<T>` with `Items` + pagination | Reduces boilerplate — same shape for all grids |
| Tenant context | `TenantRequest` base class | `TenantEndpoint` base class extracts tenant from JWT | Actions uses endpoint base class, not request base class |

| Request base hierarchy | `KeyedDTO` → `NamedDTO` → `EntityDTO` → `TenantRequest` → `SaveRequest` | `KeyedDTO` → `NamedDTO` → `SaveRequest` (no `EntityDTO`/`TenantRequest` layers) | Simpler hierarchy; tenant context from TenantEndpoint |
| Validator base | `TenantRequestValidator<T>` | `Validator<T>` (FastEndpoints default) | No `TenantRequest` base; validators don't need tenant-specific validation |
| PagedRequest | `Skip`/`Take` | `Page`/`PageSize` | More natural for frontend pagination controls |
| Contracts path | `Contracts/Features/{Group}/{Entity}/` | `Contracts/{Entity}/` (flat) | Fewer features; flat layout preferred |
| Delete request naming | `Delete{Entity}Request` (singular) | `Delete{Entities}Request` (plural) | Reflects batch semantics (deletes multiple entities) |

sections.title: Error Response Shape (RFC 7807)
content: All API errors return RFC 7807 Problem Details JSON. The frontend `parseApiError()` utility (see [Feature Service — Error Response Handling](../web/feature-service.md#error-response-handling)) parses this shape.
Returned by FastEndpoints when `FluentValidation` fails on the request:
"Slug": ["Slug must be lowercase.", "Slug must be 100 characters or fewer."]
Returned by `SendProblemAsync()` when a `CommandResult.Fail()` propagates through the endpoint:
Returned by `ProblemDetailsService` for unexpected exceptions. Never exposes stack traces or internal details:
"title": "An error occurred while processing your request.",
| `title` | Always | HTTP status phrase or validation summary |
| `detail` | Domain errors only | Human-readable error message from `CommandResult.Fail()` |
| `errorCode` | Domain errors only | Machine-readable code (e.g., `ACTION-001`) for programmatic handling |
| `errors` | Validation errors only | Dictionary of field → message arrays |
The web `ProblemDetails` interface and `parseApiError()` utility in `core/utils/api-error.util.ts` match this shape:
See [Feature Service — Error Response Handling](../web/feature-service.md#error-response-handling) for the full parsing utility.
